monitoring_reports.comments_or_issues: Total Suspended Solids (TSS) exceeded EPA limits at 4 sites (SW01, SW03, SW07, SW13) during specific dates. Nitrate levels were consistently above EPA’s optimal threshold (5 mg/L), with peaks of 47 mg/L and 49 mg/L. Phosphate levels exceeded limits at SW03 and SW13, indicating possible nutrient pollution. T-3 and T-6 showed sedimentation rates far above the EPA threshold (15 mg/cm²/day), with T-6 peaking at 168.27 mg/cm²/day. T-3 remained non-compliant across all rounds despite a declining trend. T-6 was not retrievable in Round 2 due to its location within an active silt screen.

monitoring_reports.report_name: Environmental and Social Management Plan for the Establishment of the Island Waste Resource Management Centre with Aerobic Technology using Composting Machine at Guraidhoo, Thaa Atoll
monitoring_reports.comments_or_issues: The establishment of an IWRMC with Aerobic Technology using a composting machine at Th. Guraidhoo was completed on September 12, 2024. The project was undertaken by Vehi-Con Builders Pvt Ltd. ESMP monitoring confirmed that the construction was carried out in accordance with the ESMP, with most of the agreed mitigation measures implemented satisfactorily. Some non-compliance issues were identified during the construction phase but were resolved. Additionally, minor issues that arose were addressed before the completion of construction. All required firefighting equipment has been procured and installed at the facility. No public grievances were reported during the construction phase. Vegetation clearance was conducted within the IWRMC plot as part of the construction process. Following the completion of civil works and the contractor’s demobilization, the facility was officially handed over to the Th. Guraidhoo Council for operation on September 29, 2024. The center has been registered with URA, and an application for a waste management permit has been submitted. The Island Council, as the operator, is responsible for operational phase monitoring, which is scheduled to be conducted six months after the start of operations.

ESMP monitoring confirmed that the construction was carried out in accordance with the ESMP, with most of the agreed mitigation measures implemented satisfactorily. Some non-compliance issues were identified during the construction phase but were resolved. Additionally, minor issues that arose were addressed before the completion of construction.

All required firefighting equipment has been procured and installed at the facility. No public grievances were reported during the construction phase. Vegetation clearance was conducted within the IWRMC plot as part of the construction process.

Following the completion of civil works and the contractor’s demobilization, the facility was officially handed over to the Th. Guraidhoo Council for operation on September 29, 2024. The center has been registered with URA, and an application for a waste management permit has been submitted. The Island Council, as the operator, is responsible for operational phase monitoring, which is scheduled to be conducted six months after the start of operations.

monitoring_reports.comments_or_issues: This is the second environmental monitoring report of the EIA for the proposed construction of a 13-storey mixed use residential complex with basement at Lot N5-24, Hulhumale’ phase 2. activities. Currently, no work is ongoing at the project site, as the laborers have been temporarily transferred to complete other buildings being developed by the proponent. Monitoring data collection was conducted from 19th to 22nd January 2025. Environmental components such as groundwater quality, air quality, noise levels, traffic flow and volume and solid waste management practices were monitored. The monitoring reports show improvements in key environmental parameters such as air quality, TDS, and temperature, with a few areas requiring attention, such as the gradual acidification of pH levels and the continuing reduction in salinity. Traffic volume and noise levels have increased, primarily due to construction activities and vehicle movements. It is recommended to continue monitoring these variables to ensure sustainable environmental management and mitigate any negative impacts.

monitoring_reports.report_name: EMR for the Proposed Establishment of Island Waste Resources Management Centre at Buruni, Thaa Atoll - Jan 2025
monitoring_reports.proponent: Ministry of Climate Change, Environment and Energy
monitoring_reports.comments_or_issues: Establishment of an IWRMC with aerobic technology using composting machine at Th. Buruni was completed on 15th August 2024. The site condition was well managed throughout the construction phase of the project with no major non-compliance issues being noticed during monitoring. The IWRMC plot had 23 vegetation, corresponding to 22 coconut palms and 1 tree. Out of these, 6 inferior coconut palms and the tree were publicly owned, therefore, not requiring compensation. For the remaining 16 private owned coconut palms, compensation was issued to the respective owners. No grievances were filed during the construction period and site visits by the supervision team of Riyan. Temperature, physical appearance, pH, Electrical conductivity, Total Dissolved Solid (TDS) and Salinity were tested by using Hanna Multiparameter while Nitrate was tested at MWSC Water Quality Assurance Laboratory. Most of the parameters are within the normal range. Temperature was a bit higher than the optimal range, this possibility due to the fact that the water sampled being taken might affected by the hot weather. No major issues identified

monitoring_reports.report_name: EMR 2 Construction Stage- RECLAMATION AND SHORE PROTECTION WORKS AT ALIBEY LAGOON- KAHANBUFLAHU
monitoring_reports.comments_or_issues: Reclamation works started on 27th October 2024. Nearly all sites recorded daily sedimentation rates exceeding the baseline. Sites ST1 and ST6 had daily sedimendation rates surpassing the maximum allowable thresholds. It was stated that high sedimentation at these sites is likely attributed to the rough whether conditions experienced during the monitoring period and that further investigation is necessary to rule out any project related contributions to sedimentation. It was recommended to assess the effectiveness of current mitigation measures and ensure that the bundsand silt screen are intact and functioning properly before resuming dredging activities.

monitoring_reports.report_name: Construction and Operations of Powerhouse at Felidhoo, Vaavu Atoll
monitoring_reports.comments_or_issues: The construction of the new powerhouse at V. Felidhoo, operated by STELCO, was completed on 15 November 2022 and is now operational. Environmental monitoring conducted on 10 November 2024 indicates compliance with EIA requirements, with no major environmental deviations. Noise levels and ambient air quality match the control site and EIA baseline. However, there is a slight deterioration in groundwater quality compared to the baseline. The report recommends installing a Foam Pouring and Drenching System at the fuel storage tanks, establishing a grievance redress mechanism for public and employee concerns, and ensuring operators in the generator room use earmuffs. The next monitoring is scheduled for July 2025.

monitoring_reports.report_name: MTL EMR -Sep-Oct 2024.Coral Relocation 5th Report – May 2024 to October 2024
monitoring_reports.comments_or_issues: Toxic gas levels at all monitoring sites were within the recommended standard concentrations. Though some of the sites, especially at PM2.5 and PM10 showed increased concentrations of particulates, this does not reflect a 24 hour average of the site. It is often the case that particulate concentrations level reduces drastically during late night and early morning hours, when there is less traffic on the roads surrounding the site.AFCONS did not report any grievances or concerns received from the public or internal sources during this monitoring period. However, some actions taken by the contractor based on internal inspection visits. This includes continuous clean-up of any debris on the reef and some repair work on the roads of vilingili. Majority of the marine water quality parameters were within optimal range. Nitrate levels were higher than recommended levels but was still similar to the results achieved during the baseline as well. No incidents related to either ground water or sea water contamination was reported, nor was it identified during our surveys

Phosphate levels exceeded limits at SW03 and SW13, indicating possible nutrient pollution. T-3 and T-6 showed sedimentation rates far above the EPA threshold (15 mg/cm²/day), with T-6 peaking at 168.27 mg/cm²/day.
T-3 remained non-compliant across all rounds despite a declining trend.
T-6 was not retrievable in Round 2 due to its location within an active silt screen.

raw.c10:
The establishment of an IWRMC with Aerobic Technology using a composting machine at Th. Guraidhoo was completed on September 12, 2024. The project was undertaken by Vehi-Con Builders Pvt Ltd.

ESMP monitoring confirmed that the construction was carried out in accordance with the ESMP, with most of the agreed mitigation measures implemented satisfactorily. Some non-compliance issues were identified during the construction phase but were resolved. Additionally, minor issues that arose were addressed before the completion of construction.

All required firefighting equipment has been procured and installed at the facility. No public grievances were reported during the construction phase. Vegetation clearance was conducted within the IWRMC plot as part of the construction process.

Following the completion of civil works and the contractor’s demobilization, the facility was officially handed over to the Th. Guraidhoo Council for operation on September 29, 2024. The center has been registered with URA, and an application for a waste management permit has been submitted. The Island Council, as the operator, is responsible for operational phase monitoring, which is scheduled to be conducted six months after the start of operations.

Currently, no work is ongoing at the project site, as the laborers have been temporarily transferred to complete other buildings being developed by the proponent. Monitoring data collection was conducted from 19th to 22nd January 2025. Environmental components such as groundwater quality, air quality, noise levels, traffic flow and volume and solid waste management practices were monitored. The monitoring reports show improvements in key environmental parameters such as air quality, TDS, and temperature, with a few areas requiring attention, such as the gradual acidification of pH levels and the continuing reduction in salinity. Traffic volume and noise levels have increased, primarily due to construction activities and vehicle movements. It is recommended to continue monitoring these variables to ensure sustainable environmental management and mitigate any negative impacts.

raw.c10: Establishment of an IWRMC with aerobic technology using composting machine at Th. Buruni was completed on 15th August 2024. No grievances were filed during the construction period and site visits by the supervision team of Riyan. Temperature was a bit higher than the optimal range, this possibility due to the fact that the water sampled being taken might affected by the hot weather. No major issues identified

raw.c4: Construction and Operations of Powerhouse at Felidhoo, Vaavu Atoll
raw.c10: The construction of the new powerhouse at V. Felidhoo, operated by STELCO, was completed on 15 November 2022 and is now operational. Environmental monitoring conducted on 10 November 2024 indicates compliance with EIA requirements, with no major environmental deviations. Noise levels and ambient air quality match the control site and EIA baseline. However, there is a slight deterioration in groundwater quality compared to the baseline. The report recommends installing a Foam Pouring and Drenching System at the fuel storage tanks, establishing a grievance redress mechanism for public and employee concerns, and ensuring operators in the generator room use earmuffs. The next monitoring is scheduled for July 2025.
